<!--
Thanks for contributing to Lekhini! A short, focused PR is much
easier to review than a sprawling one — please split unrelated
changes into separate PRs.
-->

## Summary

<!--
What does this PR do, and why? One or two sentences. If it fixes an
existing issue, link it: `Fixes #123`.
-->

## Type of change

<!-- Tick all that apply. -->

- [ ] `feat` — new user-visible feature
- [ ] `fix` — bug fix
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `refactor` — code change with no behaviour change
- [ ] `perf` — performance improvement
- [ ] `chore` — build / tooling / dependency bump
- [ ] `style` — formatting / no behaviour change

## How was this tested?

<!--
Lekhini is a UI app — typecheck alone won't catch visual regressions.
Tell us what you did to make sure this actually works:
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` passes
- [ ] Ran `npm run dev` and exercised the affected tool / surface
- [ ] Verified across both toolbar orientations (vertical + horizontal)
- [ ] Verified on multi-monitor (if relevant)
- [ ] Verified light + dark themes (if UI-touching)
- [ ] N/A — explain why:
-->

## Screenshots / recording

<!--
For any UI or interaction change, please attach a before/after
screenshot or a short screen recording. Drag and drop works.
-->

## Changelog

<!--
If your change is user-visible, add a line under `[Unreleased]` in
CHANGELOG.md. Maintainer-only / refactor changes can skip this.
-->

- [ ] Added an entry under `[Unreleased]` in `CHANGELOG.md`
- [ ] N/A — not user-visible

## Checklist

- [ ] My commits follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org)
- [ ] I have read [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md) and
      [CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md](../CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [ ] This PR contains one logical change (split it if not)
